'''Paul Sterling''' (born 2 August 1964) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played at representative level for [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]], and at club level for [[Hull FC]], [[Leeds Rhinos]] and the [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet Hawks]] as a {{rlp|wg}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Sterling was born in [[Wolverhampton]], [[Staffordshire, England]].

==Career==
Sterling started his rugby league career in 1993, joining [[Hull F.C.]] from [[rugby union]] club [[Bradford & Bingley RFC|Bradford & Bingley]]. After spending the 1996 season on loan at [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet Hawks]], he joined [[Leeds Rhinos]]. Sterling was the top [[Try (rugby)|try]] scorer for Leeds Rhinos during [[1997 Leeds Rhinos season|1997]]'s [[Super League II]].

Sterling won a [[Cap (sport)|cap]] for England while at Leeds in 1999 against France. He was also selected for Wales in the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]]<ref name="2000 Rugby League World Cup Squads at sportinglife.com">{{cite web|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/worldcup2000/squads/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20010617012022/http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/worldcup2000/squads/|url-status=dead|archive-date=2001-06-17|title=2000 Rugby League World Cup Squads at sportinglife.com|publisher=sportinglife.com|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref> earning [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s against Cook Islands, Lebanon, New Zealand, and Papua New Guinea, at 36 years of age, he was the oldest player in the tournament.{{citation needed|date=March 2026}}

In September 2000, Sterling took Leeds to an employment tribunal, claiming that he had been discriminated against by head coach [[Dean Lance]] earlier in the year. In December 2000, Leeds were ordered to pay Sterling £16,000 as compensation and to offer him a new one-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Wilson|first1=Andy|title=Leeds pay Sterling £16,000 for 'unconscious' race discrimination|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/story/0,3604,412167,00.html|website=The Guardian|accessdate=22 June 2014|date=16 December 2000}}</ref> Leeds intended to appeal against this ruling,<ref>{{cite web|title=Leeds hit by Sterling shock|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/1083427.stm|website=BBC Sport|accessdate=22 June 2014|date=22 December 2000}}</ref> but in April 2001, Sterling announced his retirement, stating that he had lost the desire to play the sport due to the incident.<ref>{{cite web|title=Sterling quits in disgust|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/1274267.stm|website=BBC Sport|accessdate=22 June 2014|date=12 April 2001}}</ref>
